rationale_for_baseline_tier: Facial erysipelas with systemic toxicity near the orbit/CNS needs urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment (likely IV antibiotics, sepsis screen, orbital exclusion) which a telehealth provider cannot deliver; escalate to T5 for orbital, necrotising or sepsis features.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Toxic facial erysipelas (high fever, rigors, rapidly advancing facial plaque)
escalation_edges.rationale: Systemic toxicity on the face needs urgent antibiotics (likely IV) and sepsis assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: This facial infection with a high fever needs to be seen in person today — go to the emergency department now for antibiotics.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Eye pain, double vision, pain on eye movement, vision change, confusion, purple/black blisters or numb skin
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals orbital cellulitis/cavernous sinus thrombosis or necrotising infection — emergencies.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness reaches your eyelids, you get eye pain, double vision or pain moving your eyes, become confused, or see purple/black blisters or numb skin — call 000 immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Spouse to arrange urgent transport and monitor for eye/neurological changes.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Go to the emergency department now — this facial infection with a high fever needs urgent antibiotics, likely into a vein. Keep your head elevated on the way. Call 000 for any eye pain, double vision, confusion or worsening fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: This is an after-hours emergency — attend ED / call 000 now.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document toxic facial erysipelas, antibiotics commenced, orbital/necrotising features screened, and urgent in-person/ED escalation.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Home oral antibiotics/routine follow-up for a toxic facial erysipelas without in-person assessment or orbital/necrotising screen.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Orbital cellulitis/cavernous sinus thrombosis or sepsis progresses — vision loss or death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Urgent GP without ED-level assessment despite systemic toxicity and facial location.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance is appropriate if orbital/necrotising/sepsis features; otherwise same-day ED (T4) suffices.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot examine the eyes/orbit or give IV antibiotics remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate a toxic facial erysipelas to urgent in-person/ED assessment and safety-net for orbital/necrotising/sepsis features.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Remote oral antibiotics with routine follow-up, or anchoring on a lupus malar rash.
